sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 30-year-old male, presented with a complaint of knee pain and swelling after a skiing accident on Saturday.
He described a cartwheel maneuver that resulted in a pop in his right knee and subsequent pain.
He has been taking Advil and applying ice, but the pain has been persistent.
Past medical history: The patient has no prior knee injuries and no known medical conditions.
He does not have a history of smoking, alcohol or drug use, and his occupation and travel history are not relevant to the current presentation.
Review of systems: The patient has no additional symptoms in other organs that are relevant to the initial presentation.
Current medications: I prescribed Advil as needed for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs were within normal limits: temperature 97.9°F, heart rate 64, respirations 16, blood pressure 110/74, and oxygen saturation 98%.
Physical exam: The patient has edema and ecchymosis around the right knee, positive pain to palpation, and limited range of motion with flexion and extension.
The patient reported pain with flexion and extension, but had a full range of motion.
Test Results: X-ray of the right knee showed no fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a knee contusion.
Problem list: Knee contusion; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I instructed the patient to rest the knee, apply ice 2-3 times a day for 20 minutes, and take NSAIDs (ibuprofen) every 6-8 hours as needed.
I also ordered an x-ray of the right shoulder to evaluate for potential injury.
We will reassess the patient's condition and consider physical therapy if necessary.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up in the office to reevaluate the knee and shoulder injuries.
